# tsb_oidc (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `config` (Attributes) OIDC settings for the OIDC provider. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config))
- `name` (String) Short name of the OIDC resource.
- `parent` (String) The parent id for the OIDC resource.
- `secret` (String) Base64 encoded client secret for the OIDC provider.

### Optional

- `config_generation_metadata` (Attributes) Default metadata values that will be propagated to the children Istio generated configurations. When using YAML APIs like `tctl` or `gitops`, put them into the `metadata.labels` or `metadata.annotations` instead. This field is only necessary when using gRPC APIs directly. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config_generation_metadata))
- `deletion_protection_enabled` (Boolean) When set, prevents the resource from being deleted. In order to delete the resource this property needs to be set to `false` first.
- `description` (String) A description of the resource.
- `display_name` (String) User friendly name for the resource.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Fully-qualified name of the OIDC resource.

<a id="nestedatt--config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config`

Required:

- `client_id` (String) The client ID from the OIDC provider's application configuration settings.
- `provider_config` (Attributes) OIDC provider configuration. Either dynamic or static configuration can be used. When dynamic configuration is set the TSB operator will configure OIDC settings discovered through the provider's configuration endpoint. If the provider doesn't have a configuration endpoint you can set the required OIDC settings using static values. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config--provider_config))
- `redirect_uri` (String) The public URI where TSB is accessed. This is the location where the OIDC provider will redirect after authentication and must be registered with the OIDC provider. TSB requires that the path matches /iam/v2/oidc/callback. For example, if TSB is accessed via https://example.com, then this setting should be https://example.com/iam/v2/oidc/callback and the OIDC provider application setting for the Redirect URI must match this.

Optional:

- `authorization_params` (Map of String) Optional parameters that will be included in the authorization request to the authorization endpoint. This provides a way to add non standard or optional query parameters to the authorization request. Required parameters such as "client_id", "scope, "state" and "redirect_uri" will take precedence over any parameters defined here. In other words, setting any of these parameters here will not have any effect and will be replaced by other TSB configuration.
- `jit_provisioning` (Attributes) Optional OIDC Just-In-Time (JIT) user provisioning settings. When enabled, an OIDC-authenticated subject with no existing TSB principal is auto-created from the token claims on first login. Inert unless `jit_provisioning.enabled` is set. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config--jit_provisioning))
- `max_expiration_seconds` (Number) Optional max expiration time of issued tokens. When greater than 0 this sets an upper bounds on the token expiration. If not provided or if the value is greater than the token expiration issued by the OIDC provider then the OIDC provider expiration time takes precedence.
- `offline_access_config` (Attributes) Optional OIDC settings specific to offline access. When specified these settings take precedence over top-level OIDC settings. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config--offline_access_config))
- `scopes` (List of String) Scopes passed to the OIDC provider in the Authentication Request. Required scope 'openid' is included by default, any additional scopes will be appended in the Authorization Request. Additional scopes such as 'profile' or 'email' are generally required if user records in TSB can not be identified with the ID Token 'sub' claim alone.

<a id="nestedatt--config--provider_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config.provider_config`

Optional:

- `dynamic` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config--provider_config--dynamic))
- `static` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config--provider_config--static))

<a id="nestedatt--config--provider_config--dynamic"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config.provider_config.dynamic`

Required:

- `configuration_uri` (String) OIDC provider's well-known OIDC configuration URI. When set TSB will automatically configure the OIDC client settings for the Authorization Endpoint, Token Endpoint and JWKS URI from the OIDC Provider's configuration URI.


<a id="nestedatt--config--provider_config--static"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config.provider_config.static`

Required:

- `authorization_endpoint` (String) The Authorization Endpoint for the OIDC provider.
- `token_endpoint` (String) The Token Endpoint for the OIDC provider.

Optional:

- `device_code_endpoint` (String) The Device Code endpoint for the OIDC provider. This is optional but required when using the Device Code authentication flow.
- `introspection_endpoint` (String) The Introspection endpoint for the OIDC provider. This is optional and used as an authentication source for the Token Exchange flow.
- `jwks` (String) JSON string with the OIDC provider's JSON Web Key Sets. In general the URI for the Key Set is the preferred method for configuring JWKS. This setting is provided in case the provider doesn't publish JWKS via a public URI.
- `jwks_uri` (String) URI for the OIDC provider's JSON Web Key Sets. This can be found in the OIDC provider's configuration response. The JWKS are used for token verification.



<a id="nestedatt--config--jit_provisioning"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config.jit_provisioning`

Optional:

- `enabled` (Boolean) Enabled turns on JIT user creation: an OIDC-authenticated subject with no existing TSB principal is auto-created from the token claims.
- `group_sync` (Attributes) GroupSync controls reconciliation of OIDC group claims into TSB team membership. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config--jit_provisioning--group_sync))
- `groups_claim` (String) GroupsClaim selects which OIDC token claim carries the user's group memberships, matched against each team's `oidc_groups` during reconciliation. Defaults to "groups". Ignored for providers whose groups are resolved via an external directory (GroupSync.group_source) rather than a token claim.
- `provider_name` (String) ProviderName is recorded on the created user's `provider_name` field, identifying which configured identity provider the user originated from (for example "google", "azure-oidc", or "pingam"). Empty leaves the field unset.
- `subject_claim` (String) SubjectClaim selects which OIDC claim becomes the TSB principal name. Valid values: "sub" (default; recommended), "email", "preferred_username", and "email-prefix" (the local part of the "email" claim, preserved for backward compatibility with the legacy Google behavior). An empty value defaults to "sub".

<a id="nestedatt--config--jit_provisioning--group_sync"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config.jit_provisioning.group_sync`

Optional:

- `google_admin_sdk` (Attributes) Google Admin SDK Directory API, used for Google Workspace. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config--jit_provisioning--group_sync--google_admin_sdk))
- `max_drift_window` (Attributes) MaxDriftWindow bounds how long IdP-to-TSB membership may diverge and also clamps the OIDC bearer lifetime. Clamped to the range [15m, 24h] (snapped to the nearest bound); defaults to 24h. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config--jit_provisioning--group_sync--max_drift_window))
- `mode` (String) Mode selects the group-reconciliation behavior. Defaults to OFF.
- `require_mapped_team` (Boolean) RequireMappedTeam, when true, denies login or refresh for users whose group claims resolve to no existing teams (break-glass teams are exempt). Only meaningful when `mode` is not OFF.

<a id="nestedatt--config--jit_provisioning--group_sync--google_admin_sdk"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config.jit_provisioning.group_sync.google_admin_sdk`

Optional:

- `impersonate_subject` (String) ImpersonateSubject is the Google Workspace administrator that the service account impersonates via domain-wide delegation.
- `scopes` (List of String) Scopes are the OAuth scopes requested. Defaults to a single scope, https://www.googleapis.com/auth/admin.directory.group.readonly.
- `service_account_secret_ref` (String) ServiceAccountSecretRef references the mounted service-account JSON credential.


<a id="nestedatt--config--jit_provisioning--group_sync--max_drift_window"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config.jit_provisioning.group_sync.max_drift_window`

Optional:

- `nanos` (Number) Signed fractions of a second at nanosecond resolution of the span of time. Durations less than one second are represented with a 0 `seconds` field and a positive or negative `nanos` field. For durations of one second or more, a non-zero value for the `nanos` field must be of the same sign as the `seconds` field. Must be from -999,999,999 to +999,999,999 inclusive.
- `seconds` (Number) Signed seconds of the span of time. Must be from -315,576,000,000 to +315,576,000,000 inclusive. Note: these bounds are computed from: 60 sec/min * 60 min/hr * 24 hr/day * 365.25 days/year * 10000 years




<a id="nestedatt--config--offline_access_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config.offline_access_config`

Optional:

- `device_code_auth` (Attributes) OIDC settings for Device Code Authorization grant used with offline access. Any settings applied here override top-level OIDC configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config--offline_access_config--device_code_auth))
- `token_exchange` (Attributes) OIDC settings for Token Exchange grant used with offline access. Any settings applied here override top-level OIDC configuration. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config--offline_access_config--token_exchange))

<a id="nestedatt--config--offline_access_config--device_code_auth"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config.offline_access_config.device_code_auth`

Optional:

- `client_id` (String) The client ID from the OIDC provider's application configuration settings.
- `provider_config` (Attributes) OIDC provider configuration. Either dynamic or static configuration can be used. When dynamic configuration is set the TSB operator will configure OIDC settings discovered through the provider's configuration endpoint. If the provider doesn't have a configuration endpoint you can set the required OIDC settings using static values. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config--offline_access_config--device_code_auth--provider_config))
- `scopes` (List of String) Scopes passed to the OIDC provider in the Device Code request Required scope 'openid' is included by default, any additional scopes will be appended in the Device Code Authorization request. Additional scopes such as 'profile' or 'email' are generally required if user records in TSB can not be identified with the ID Token 'sub' claim alone.
- `skip_client_id_check` (Boolean) Instructs JWT validation to ignore the 'aud' claim. When set to true, comparisons between the 'aud' claim in the JWT token and the 'client_id' in the OIDC provider's configuration will be skipped.

<a id="nestedatt--config--offline_access_config--device_code_auth--provider_config"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config.offline_access_config.device_code_auth.provider_config`

Optional:

- `dynamic` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config--offline_access_config--device_code_auth--provider_config--dynamic))
- `static` (Attributes) (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config--offline_access_config--device_code_auth--provider_config--static))

<a id="nestedatt--config--offline_access_config--device_code_auth--provider_config--dynamic"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config.offline_access_config.device_code_auth.provider_config.dynamic`

Required:

- `configuration_uri` (String) OIDC provider's well-known OIDC configuration URI. When set TSB will automatically configure the OIDC client settings for the Authorization Endpoint, Token Endpoint and JWKS URI from the OIDC Provider's configuration URI.


<a id="nestedatt--config--offline_access_config--device_code_auth--provider_config--static"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config.offline_access_config.device_code_auth.provider_config.static`

Required:

- `authorization_endpoint` (String) The Authorization Endpoint for the OIDC provider.
- `token_endpoint` (String) The Token Endpoint for the OIDC provider.

Optional:

- `device_code_endpoint` (String) The Device Code endpoint for the OIDC provider. This is optional but required when using the Device Code authentication flow.
- `introspection_endpoint` (String) The Introspection endpoint for the OIDC provider. This is optional and used as an authentication source for the Token Exchange flow.
- `jwks` (String) JSON string with the OIDC provider's JSON Web Key Sets. In general the URI for the Key Set is the preferred method for configuring JWKS. This setting is provided in case the provider doesn't publish JWKS via a public URI.
- `jwks_uri` (String) URI for the OIDC provider's JSON Web Key Sets. This can be found in the OIDC provider's configuration response. The JWKS are used for token verification.




<a id="nestedatt--config--offline_access_config--token_exchange"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config.offline_access_config.token_exchange`

Optional:

- `client_id` (String) The client ID from the OIDC provider's application configuration settings.
- `provider_config` (Attributes) OIDC provider configuration. Either dynamic or static configuration can be used. When dynamic configuration is set the TSB operator will configure OIDC settings discovered through the provider's configuration endpoint. If the provider doesn't have a configuration endpoint you can set the required OIDC settings using static values. (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--config--offline_access_config--token_exchange--provider_config))
- `scopes` (List of String) Scopes passed to the OIDC provider in the Device Code request Required scope 'openid' is included by default, any additional scopes will be appended in the Device Code Authorization request. Additional scopes such as 'profile' or 'email' are generally required if user records in TSB can not be identified with the ID Token 'sub' claim alone.
- `skip_client_id_check` (Boolean) Instructs JWT validation to ignore the 'aud' claim. When set to true, comparisons between the 'aud' claim in the JWT token and the 'client_id' in the OIDC provider's configuration will be skipped.

<a id="nestedatt--config--offline_access_config--token_exchange--provider_config"></a>

- `configuration_uri` (String) OIDC provider's well-known OIDC configuration URI. When set TSB will automatically configure the OIDC client settings for the Authorization Endpoint, Token Endpoint and JWKS URI from the OIDC Provider's configuration URI.

Optional:

- `device_code_endpoint` (String) The Device Code endpoint for the OIDC provider. This is optional but required when using the Device Code authentication flow.
- `introspection_endpoint` (String) The Introspection endpoint for the OIDC provider. This is optional and used as an authentication source for the Token Exchange flow.
- `jwks` (String) JSON string with the OIDC provider's JSON Web Key Sets. In general the URI for the Key Set is the preferred method for configuring JWKS. This setting is provided in case the provider doesn't publish JWKS via a public URI.
- `jwks_uri` (String) URI for the OIDC provider's JSON Web Key Sets. This can be found in the OIDC provider's configuration response. The JWKS are used for token verification.






<a id="nestedatt--config_generation_metadata"></a>
### Nested Schema for `config_generation_metadata`

Optional:

- `annotations` (Map of String) Set of key value paris that will be added into the `metadata.annotations` field of the Istio generated configurations.
- `labels` (Map of String) Set of key value paris that will be added into the `metadata.labels` field of the Istio generated configurations.
